UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: A user's profile information within the SquadForge application, including personal details and preferences.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

fullName.description: The full name of the user.

avatarUrl.description: URL to the user's optional avatar image.

notificationsEnabled.description: Indicates if the user has globally enabled push notifications.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

Team.title: Team
Team.description: Represents a sports team within the SquadForge application, managed by users.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Team entity.

sport.description: The sport associated with the team.

teamLogoUrl.description: URL to the team's optional logo image.

teamCode.description: A unique 6-character alphanumeric code used for joining the team.

createdBy.description: Reference to the UserProfile ID of the user who created this team. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Team)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the team was created.

TeamMember.title: TeamMember
TeamMember.description: Represents a specific user's membership within a team, including their role and team-specific details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TeamMember entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile ID of the member. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N TeamMember)

teamId.description: Reference to the Team ID this member belongs to. (Relationship: Team 1:N TeamMember)

role.description: The member's role within the team (e.g., 'Admin', 'Member').

position.description: The member's position on the team (optional).

joinedAt.description: Timestamp when the user joined this specific team.

Post.title: Post
Post.description: An entry in a team's activity feed, which can include text, an image, or a linked event.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Post entity.

teamId.description: Reference to the Team ID this post belongs to. (Relationship: Team 1:N Post)

authorId.description: Reference to the UserProfile ID of the user who authored this post. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Post)

content.description: The main text content of the post.

imageUrl.description: URL to an optional single image attached to the post.

linkedEventId.description: Optional reference to an Event ID if the post is linking to an event. (Relationship: Event 1:N Post)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the post was created.

isSystemPost.description: Indicates if the post was automatically generated by the system (e.g., event created, member joined).

Comment.title: Comment
Comment.description: A comment made on a specific post in the team feed.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Comment entity.

postId.description: Reference to the Post ID this comment belongs to. (Relationship: Post 1:N Comment)

authorId.description: Reference to the UserProfile ID of the user who authored this comment. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Comment)

content.description: The text content of the comment.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the comment was created.

Event.title: Event
Event.description: A scheduled event for a team, created by an admin.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Event entity.

teamId.description: Reference to the Team ID this event belongs to. (Relationship: Team 1:N Event)

startTime.description: The start time of the event.

endTime.description: The optional end time of the event.

location.description: The location where the event will take place.

description.description: A detailed description of the event.

createdBy.description: Reference to the UserProfile ID of the admin who created this event. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Event)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the event was created.

RSVP.title: RSVP
RSVP.description: A user's response to an event invitation, indicating their attendance status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the RSVP entity.

eventId.description: Reference to the Event ID this RSVP is for. (Relationship: Event 1:N RSVP)

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile ID of the user who provided the RSVP. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N RSVP)

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the RSVP status was last updated.

Availability.title: Availability
Availability.description: Records a user's declared unavailability for a specific date within a team.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Availability entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile ID of the user marking unavailability. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Availability)

teamId.description: Reference to the Team ID this availability applies to. (Relationship: Team 1:N Availability)

date.description: The specific date for which the user is unavailable.

status.description: The status of availability, fixed to 'unavailable'.

File.title: File
File.description: A shared file uploaded by a team member to a specific team.

id.description: Unique identifier for the File entity.

teamId.description: Reference to the Team ID this file belongs to. (Relationship: Team 1:N File)

fileUrl.description: URL to the uploaded file in storage.

fileName.description: The original name of the uploaded file.

uploadedBy.description: Reference to the UserProfile ID of the user who uploaded the file. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N File)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the file was uploaded.

GroupChat.title: GroupChat
GroupChat.description: A group messaging chat room within a specific team.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GroupChat entity.

teamId.description: Reference to the Team ID this chat belongs to. (Relationship: Team 1:N GroupChat)

name.description: The name of the group chat.

createdBy.description: Reference to the UserProfile ID of the user who created this chat. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N GroupChat)

memberIds.description: An array of UserProfile IDs of members participating in this chat. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N GroupChat)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the group chat was created.

isDeleted.description: Boolean flag indicating if the chat has been globally deleted by an admin. False by default.

ChatMessage.title: ChatMessage
ChatMessage.description: An individual message or poll within a group chat.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ChatMessage entity.

chatId.description: Reference to the GroupChat ID this message belongs to. (Relationship: GroupChat 1:N ChatMessage)

authorId.description: Reference to the UserProfile ID of the user who sent this message. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ChatMessage)

content.description: The text content of the message (if type is 'text').

pollId.description: Optional reference to a Poll ID if the message type is 'poll'. (Relationship: Poll 1:N ChatMessage)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the message was sent.

UserChatHidden.title: UserChatHidden
UserChatHidden.description: Records that a specific user has hidden a group chat from their view.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserChatHidden entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile ID of the user who hid the chat. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserChatHidden)

chatId.description: Reference to the GroupChat ID that was hidden. (Relationship: GroupChat 1:N UserChatHidden)

hiddenAt.description: Timestamp when the user hid the chat.

Poll.title: Poll
Poll.description: A poll created within a group chat for decision-making.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Poll entity.

chatId.description: Reference to the GroupChat ID this poll belongs to. (Relationship: GroupChat 1:N Poll)

question.description: The question posed in the poll.

options.description: An array of strings representing the possible options for the poll (minimum 2, maximum 6).

createdBy.description: Reference to the UserProfile ID of the user who created this poll. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Poll)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the poll was created.

isClosed.description: Boolean flag indicating if the poll is closed and no longer accepting votes.

Vote.title: Vote
Vote.description: A user's selection for a specific poll option.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Vote entity.

pollId.description: Reference to the Poll ID this vote is for. (Relationship: Poll 1:N Vote)

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile ID of the user who cast the vote. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Vote)

selectedOptionIndex.description: The zero-based index of the chosen option from the Poll's options array.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the vote was cast or last changed.

definition.description: Stores user's private profile information. The document ID (`userId`) matches the user's authentication UID. Access is restricted to the owner (`request.auth.uid == userId`).

Stores core team information and metadata. The document includes a denormalized 'teamAdminIds' array for efficient admin access checks at the team level. Read access is allowed for validating 'teamCode' during joining, otherwise restricted to team members. Write access is restricted to team admins.

Stores a specific user's membership details and role ('Admin' or 'Member') within a team. The document ID (`userId`) matches the user's UID. All team members can read these documents. A user can update their own document, and team admins can update any member's document in their team. Crucial for establishing team-scoped authorization.

params.name: teamId
params.description: The unique identifier of the team.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (team member).

definition.description: A sentinel collection containing a document for each user who is an 'Admin' of the associated team. Used for efficient security rule checks (`exists()`) for admin privileges within a team, avoiding `get()` calls. This collection is kept in sync with the 'role' field in the 'teamMembers' subcollection.

params.name: teamId
params.description: The unique identifier of the team.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (team admin).

definition.description: Stores individual posts for a team's activity feed. Includes `teamId` and `authorId`. Read/create access for all team members. Update/delete access by the author or a team admin.

params.name: teamId
params.description: The unique identifier of the team.

params.name: postId
params.description: The unique identifier of the post.

definition.description: Stores comments made on specific team posts. Includes `teamId`, `postId`, and `authorId`. Read/create access for all team members. Update/delete access by the author or a team admin.

params.name: teamId
params.description: The unique identifier of the team.

params.name: postId
params.description: The unique identifier of the post the comment belongs to.

params.name: commentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the comment.

definition.description: Stores scheduled events for a team. Includes `teamId` and `createdBy`. Read access for all team members. Create/update/delete access restricted to team admins.

params.name: teamId
params.description: The unique identifier of the team.

params.name: eventId
params.description: The unique identifier of the event.

definition.description: Stores a user's RSVP status for a specific event. The document ID (`userId`) ensures only one RSVP per user per event. Includes `teamId`, `eventId`, and `userId`. Read access for all team members. Create/update access restricted to the `userId` associated with the RSVP.

params.name: teamId
params.description: The unique identifier of the team.

params.name: eventId
params.description: The unique identifier of the event.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who provided the RSVP.

definition.description: Records a user's declared unavailability for a specific date within a team. The document ID (`availabilityId`) can be a composite like `userId_YYYY-MM-DD` for uniqueness. Includes `teamId`, `userId`, and `date`. Read access for all team members. Create/update/delete access restricted to the `userId` associated with the availability.

params.name: teamId
params.description: The unique identifier of the team.

params.name: availabilityId
params.description: A unique identifier for the availability entry, typically a composite of userId and date.

definition.description: Stores information about files shared within a team. Includes `teamId` and `uploadedBy`. Read access for all team members. Create/delete access restricted to the uploader or a team admin.

params.name: teamId
params.description: The unique identifier of the team.

params.name: fileId
params.description: The unique identifier of the file.

definition.description: Stores information about group chats within a team. Includes `teamId`, `createdBy`, and a `memberIds` array for denormalized chat-specific authorization. The `isDeleted` flag supports global deletion by admins. Read access if `request.auth.uid` is in `memberIds` and `isDeleted` is false. Create by any team member. Update (e.g., `isDeleted` status) by the `createdBy` user or a team admin.

params.name: teamId
params.description: The unique identifier of the team.

params.name: chatId
params.description: The unique identifier of the group chat.

definition.description: Stores individual messages within a group chat. Includes `teamId`, `chatId`, `authorId`, and a denormalized `chatMemberIds` array (copied from parent `GroupChat`) for authorization independence. Read/create access if `request.auth.uid` is in `chatMemberIds`. Update/delete access restricted to the `authorId`.

params.name: teamId
params.description: The unique identifier of the team.

params.name: chatId
params.description: The unique identifier of the group chat.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique identifier of the chat message.

definition.description: Records that a specific user has hidden a group chat from their view. This is a user-private collection, with access restricted to the owner (`request.auth.uid == userId`). Used for members' 'self-delete' of chats.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who hid the chat.

params.name: chatId
params.description: The unique identifier of the group chat that was hidden.

definition.description: Stores poll information within a group chat. Includes `teamId`, `chatId`, `createdBy`, and a denormalized `chatMemberIds` array (copied from parent `GroupChat`) for authorization independence. Read/create access if `request.auth.uid` is in `chatMemberIds`. Update access (e.g., closing the poll) restricted to `createdBy` or a team admin.

params.name: teamId
params.description: The unique identifier of the team.

params.name: chatId
params.description: The unique identifier of the group chat.

params.name: pollId
params.description: The unique identifier of the poll.

definition.description: Stores a user's vote for a specific poll option. The document ID (`userId`) ensures only one vote per user per poll. Includes `teamId`, `chatId`, `pollId`, `userId`, denormalized `chatMemberIds` (copied from parent `GroupChat`), and denormalized `pollIsClosed` (copied from parent `Poll`) for authorization independence. Read access for chat members. Create/update access restricted to the `userId` if the poll is not closed.

params.name: teamId
params.description: The unique identifier of the team.

params.name: chatId
params.description: The unique identifier of the group chat.

params.name: pollId
params.description: The unique identifier of the poll.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who cast the vote.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed following the core principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC (Database Access Control), QAPs (Query-Agnostic Permissions), and Invariants. The primary goal is to minimize security rule complexity and enhance debuggability by avoiding `get()` calls in rules and ensuring all `list` operations are secure.

**1. Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
*   **Team Membership & Roles:** Instead of relying on a `get()` call to a parent `TeamMember` document to determine a user's role or membership for every operation within a team, we employ a two-pronged approach:
    *   **Membership Check:** For basic `read` access to any subcollection within a team (e.g., posts, events, files), rules will use `exists(/teams/$(teamId)/teamMembers/$(request.auth.uid))`. This is an efficient check for the presence of a membership document, confirming the user is part of the team, without fetching the document's content.
    *   **Role Check (Admin Status):** To check if a user is an 'Admin' for sensitive operations (e.g., creating events, deleting posts), a dedicated subcollection `/teams/{teamId}/admins/{userId}` is introduced. This collection contains a lightweight document for each admin. Rules can then use `exists(/teams/$(teamId)/admins/$(request.auth.uid))` to verify admin status without a `get()` call or denormalizing admin lists onto every document. Additionally, for rules specific to the `Team` document itself, the `Team` document will include a `teamAdminIds` array, which needs to be kept in sync with the `/teams/{teamId}/admins` collection via Cloud Functions.
*   **Group Chat Membership:** For `GroupChat` documents, the `memberIds` array is denormalized directly onto the document. For its subcollections (`ChatMessage`, `Poll`, `Vote`), the `chatMemberIds` array will be denormalized from the parent `GroupChat` document onto each child document. This ensures that access to chat-specific data (messages, polls, votes) can be determined directly from the `resource.data` of the document being accessed, without requiring a `get()` to the parent chat.
*   **Poll Status:** For `Vote` documents, the `pollIsClosed` status will be denormalized from the parent `Poll` document. This allows rules to prevent votes on closed polls by checking `resource.data.pollIsClosed` directly, again avoiding `get()` calls.

**2. QAPs (Query-Agnostic Permissions) for Secure List Operations:**
*   **Path-Based Ownership:** User-specific data, such as `/userProfiles/{userId}` and `/userProfiles/{userId}/hiddenChats/{chatId}`, is structured with `userId` as a top-level or sub-collection path parameter. This inherently scopes `list` queries to the authenticated user's data (`request.auth.uid == userId`), making them QAP-compliant.
*   **Team-Scoped Data:** All team-related data (posts, events, chats, etc.) are nested under `/teams/{teamId}/...`. A `list` query on any of these subcollections (e.g., `/teams/{teamId}/posts`) will implicitly filter by `teamId`. The security rules then only need to verify that `request.auth.uid` is a member of that specific `teamId` using the `exists(/teams/$(teamId)/teamMembers/$(request.auth.uid))` check. This ensures that users can only list data for teams they belong to, without requiring client-side filtering or inefficient rule evaluation.
*   **Team Discovery:** Users cannot `list` all `teams`. Instead, they can only join a team if they know its `teamCode`. Once a user has joined a team, they can retrieve their associated `TeamMember` documents via a query (e.g., `db.collectionGroup('teamMembers').where('userId', '==', request.auth.uid)`), which then gives them the `teamId`s they belong to, allowing them to construct valid, secure queries for team-specific data.

This design prioritizes embedding authorization context directly into the relevant documents or providing direct `exists()` checks via dedicated collections, significantly simplifying Firestore Security Rules and ensuring robust, scalable access control.
